title: Regulations in terms of the Traditional Authorities Act, 1995, 1997
title_en: Regulations in terms of the Traditional Authorities Act, 1995, 1997

provisions.title: Definitions
provisions.content: In these regulations, unless the context indicates otherwise, any word to which a meaning has been assigned in the Act shall have that meaning and “ the Act ” means the Traditional Authorities Act, 1995 (Act No. 17 of 1995 ). [The Traditional Authorities Act 17 of 1995 has been replaced by the Traditional Authorities Act 25 of 2000 .]

provisions.title: Remuneration of traditional leaders and secretaries
provisions.content: The remuneration payable to the different categories of traditional leaders referred to in paragraph (a) of subsection (1) of section 13 of the Act and to the secretary referred to in paragraph (b) of that subsection shall be as follows- (a) a chief, per month - N$1 000; (b) a senior traditional councillor, per month - N$750; (c) a traditional councillor, per month - N$600; and (d) a secretary, per month - N$550. History of this document 15 November 2017 this version Consolidation 01 December 1997 Published in Government Gazette 1741 Commenced Related documents [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Facebook Page Visit our Twitter/X profile About NamibLII NamibLII is a project of the Law Reform and Development Commission managed by the Directorate: Law Reform of the Ministry of Justice of the Republic of Namibia. In terms of the Law Reform and Development Commission Act, 1991 (Act No. 29 of 1991), the Commission is mandated to consolidate or codify any branch of the law or to introduce other measures aimed at making the law more readily accessible. NamibLII is the embodiment of this mandate making annotated statutes, judgments, and gazettes available in a user-friendly format.
